chief_complaint: A 62-year-old female with recurrent polymorphic ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation following acute myocardial infarction.
history: The patient developed recurrent polymorphic ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation refractory to antiarrhythmics (amiodarone/esmolol/lidocaine) and revascularization. She had a history of acute anterior wall myocardial infarction, Killip class II-III acute left heart failure, secondary hepatic dysfunction, hypertension, and type 2 diabetes mellitus.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: ECG: polymorphic ventricular tachycardia/ventricular fibrillation. Bloods: electrolyte correction. Imaging: not provided. Other tests: N-terminal B-type natriuretic peptide precursor (33,593-->9839 pg/mL), left ventricular ejection fraction (40%-->46%).
correct_diagnosis: Atrial overdrive pacing in drug-refractory postinfarction electrical storm
differentials: Atrial overdrive pacing in drug-refractory postinfarction electrical storm
Killip class II-III acute left heart failure
correct_ranking: Atrial overdrive pacing in drug-refractory postinfarction electrical storm
Killip class II-III acute left heart failure
